page: Microsoft Azure F32amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, the instance includes 3780 GB of local NVMe storage and supports Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the F32amds_v7 demonstrates strong performance characteristics. Its single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores fall within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show strong throughput, reaching 216.92 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 129.17 GB/sec for write operations. Database operations under Passmark also achieve top-tier status, while Redis SET operations reach 92.68 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F32amds_v7 offers a balanced profile for memory-heavy and storage-intensive workloads by bundling substantial local storage with a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio. This resource density reduces the need to scale out to larger instances merely to satisfy memory or local storage requirements. However, a notable tradeoff is the lack of a dedicated GPU, which limits its efficiency for heavy parallel computing tasks like large-scale deep learning, as evidenced by its average performance in 70B LLM inference benchmarks.

This server configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure F32amds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio, and includes 3780 GB of local storage. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and database operations, alongside strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. While highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads due to its dense resource allocation, the absence of a dedicated GPU makes it less suitable for large-scale machine learning acceleration. The F32amds_v7 is best suited for large databases, in-memory caching, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32amds_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and single-core CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F32amds_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage-optimized virtual machine designed for demanding database operations and high-throughput enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 256 GB of RAM and 3780 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput web serving capabilities
Optimized for large databases, in-memory caching, and data warehousing
